rationale: The previously-reserved limited partnership name being transferred. Mapped to entity.name to mirror LP_MLPA-1 (Application for Reservation of Name), where the reserved string is also entity.name. Downstream rubrics and filing.entities[0].name reference this key.

Name of the original applicant (the transferor). Reuses applicant.name from LP_MLPA-1 — the original applicant is the same role that filed the reservation, now transferring it. Avoids inventing a parallel transferor.* prefix when the existing applicant.* family already fits.
Name of the person or entity receiving the reserved name. Introduces a new transferee.* role prefix (parallel to applicant.*) for the receiving party.
Single combined-line address of the transferee. Mirrors applicant.address shape from LP_MLPA-1 (one widget, one combined string).
Date the transferor signs the notice.

Printed name and capacity of the original applicant (transferor), who signs the notice. Shape D primitive (printed_name_and_capacity per 2026-04-30-filing-signer-shape.md) applied under the applicant.* role prefix instead of filing.signer.* — exactly mirrors LP_MLPA-1, since the same role (the original applicant) signs both the reservation and the transfer.

label_verbatim: Name of entity(s) on the submitted filings [1]
rationale: Cover-letter row 1. Should match entity.name (the reserved string being transferred).

widget_id: Name of entitys on the submitted filings 2
rationale: Cover-letter row 2. Typically empty on a single-name transfer.

label_verbatim: Hold attested copy for pick up

widget_id: Contact email address for this filing
Email address to use for annual report reminders
label_verbatim: (Email address to use for annual report reminders)
rationale: Annual-report reminder email. A name transfer does not create an entity, so this field is typically blank — included for cover-letter primitive completeness.
